summary: The chemotactic cytokine primarily functions by recruiting specific types of white blood cells like neutrophils toward areas needing repair; interleukin-8 stands out due to consistent experimental validation supporting these critical cellular movement mechanisms essential during inflammation and infection response phases. This summary captures the essence while staying within specified length constraints focusing directly on core aspects pertinent to defining a 'chemotactic' function.
